headline: Construction Worker Deaths Reach 168 in Single Year
context: DOSH recorded 168 construction worker deaths in 2025 a 15% increase making construction Malaysia deadliest industry.

cards.big: 168 construction workers died on Malaysian sites in 2025 up 15%
cards.sub: Falls scaffolding collapses and machinery strikes are leading causes

cards.big: Foreign workers accounted for 72% of fatalities representing 65% of workforce
cards.sub: Language barriers and inadequate safety training in native languages are factors

cards.big: CIDB safety certification compliance dropped to 68% in 2025
cards.sub: Small contractors with fewer than 50 workers have only 42% compliance

cards.big: Malaysia fatality rate of 12.8 per 100,000 is 6 times Japan rate
cards.sub: Mandatory safety officer requirement in 2024 has not yet reduced deaths

cards.big: Worker deaths test whether safety regulations reach every construction site
168 construction worker deaths in one year. Safety regulation without site-level enforcement is meaningless.
